contentBlocks.original:
आदिलक्ष्मि
सुमनस वन्दित सुन्दरि माधवि, चन्द्र सहॊदरि हेममये
मुनिगण वन्दित मोक्षप्रदायनि, मञ्जुल भाषिणि वेदनुते ।
पङ्कजवासिनि देव सुपूजित, सद्गुण वर्षिणि शान्तियुते
जय जयहे मधुसूदन कामिनि, आदिलक्ष्मि परिपालय माम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādilakṣmi
sumanasa vandita sundari mādhavi, chandra sahodari hēmamayē
munigaṇa vandita mōkṣapradāyani, mañjula bhāṣiṇi vēdanutē ।
paṅkajavāsini dēva supūjita, sadguṇa varṣiṇi śāntiyutē
jaya jayahē madhusūdana kāmini, ādilakṣmi paripālaya mām ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Adi Lakshmi: O primal Lakshmi, saluted by all the virtuous, beautiful and divine, sister of the moon, golden-formed, revered by sages, grantor of liberation, of enchanting radiance — victory to you, O Devi Lakshmi!

contentBlocks.original:
धान्यलक्ष्मि
अयिकलि कल्मष नाशिनि कामिनि, वैदिक रूपिणि वेदमये
क्षीर समुद्भव मङ्गल रूपिणि, मन्त्रनिवासिनि मन्त्रनुते ।
मङ्गलदायिनि अम्बुजवासिनि, देवगणाश्रित पादयुते
जय जयहे मधुसूदन कामिनि, धान्यलक्ष्मि सदापालय माम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhānyalakṣmi
ayikali kalmaṣa nāśini kāmini, vaidika rūpiṇi vēdamayē
kṣīra samudbhava maṅgaḻa rūpiṇi, mantranivāsini mantranutē ।
maṅgaḻadāyini ambujavāsini, dēvagaṇāśrita pādayutē
jaya jayahē madhusūdana kāmini, dhānyalakṣmi sadāpālaya mām ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhanya Lakshmi: O destroyer of the impurities of the Kali age, gracious and beloved, of Vedic form pervading the Vedas, born of the milk-ocean, of auspicious form, dwelling within sacred mantras — victory to you, O Devi Lakshmi!

contentBlocks.original:
धैर्यलक्ष्मि
जयवरवर्षिणि वैष्णवि भार्गवि, मन्त्र स्वरूपिणि मन्त्रमये
सुरगण पूजित शीघ्र फलप्रद, ज्ञान विकासिनि शास्त्रनुते ।
भवभयहारिणि पापविमोचनि, साधु जनाश्रित पादयुते
जय जयहे मधु सूधन कामिनि, धैर्यलक्ष्मी परिपालय माम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhairyalakṣmi
jayavaravarṣiṇi vaiṣṇavi bhārgavi, mantra svarūpiṇi mantramayē
suragaṇa pūjita śīghra phalaprada, jñāna vikāsini śāstranutē ।
bhavabhayahāriṇi pāpavimōchani, sādhu janāśrita pādayutē
jaya jayahē madhu sūdhana kāmini, dhairyalakṣmī paripālaya mām ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhairya Lakshmi: O showerer of victory, O Vaishnavi, O Bhargavi, of the form of mantra pervading all mantras, swiftly granting fruits, worshipped by the hosts of gods — victory to you, O Devi Lakshmi!

contentBlocks.original:
गजलक्ष्मि
जय जय दुर्गति नाशिनि कामिनि, सर्वफलप्रद शास्त्रमये
रधगज तुरगपदाति समावृत, परिजन मण्डित लोकनुते ।
हरिहर ब्रह्म सुपूजित सेवित, ताप निवारिणि पादयुते
जय जयहे मधुसूदन कामिनि, गजलक्ष्मी रूपेण पालय माम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gajalakṣmi
jaya jaya durgati nāśini kāmini, sarvaphalaprada śāstramayē
radhagaja turagapadāti samāvṛta, parijana maṇḍita lōkanutē ।
harihara brahma supūjita sēvita, tāpa nivāriṇi pādayutē
jaya jayahē madhusūdana kāmini, gajalakṣmī rūpēṇa pālaya mām ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Gaja Lakshmi: Victory to you, O destroyer of all sorrow, O beloved one, grantor of all fruits, pervading all scriptures — attended by chariots, elephants, horses, and foot-soldiers, with devoted attendants — victory to you, O Devi Lakshmi!

contentBlocks.original:
सन्तानलक्ष्मि
अयिखग वाहिनि मोहिनि चक्रिणि, रागविवर्धिनि ज्ञानमये
गुणगणवारधि लोकहितैषिणि, स्वरसप्त भूषित गाननुते ।
सकल सुरासुर देव मुनीश्वर, मानव वन्दित पादयुते
जय जयहे मधुसूदन कामिनि, सन्तानलक्ष्मी त्वं पालय माम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
santānalakṣmi
ayikhaga vāhini mōhini chakriṇi, rāgavivardhini jñānamayē
guṇagaṇavāradhi lōkahitaiṣiṇi, svarasapta bhūṣita gānanutē ।
sakala surāsura dēva munīśvara, mānava vandita pādayutē
jaya jayahē madhusūdana kāmini, santānalakṣmī tvaṃ pālaya mām ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Santana Lakshmi: O rider of the celestial bird (Garuda), O enchantress, O wielder of the discus, increaser of love, of the nature of wisdom — ocean of virtues, ever seeking the welfare of all beings — victory to you, O Devi Lakshmi!

contentBlocks.original:
विजयलक्ष्मि
जय कमलासिनि सद्गति दायिनि, ज्ञानविकासिनि गानमये
अनुदिन मर्चित कुङ्कुम धूसर, भूषित वासित वाद्यनुते ।
कनकधरास्तुति वैभव वन्दित, शङ्करदेशिक मान्यपदे
जय जयहे मधुसूदन कामिनि, विजयलक्ष्मी सदा पालय माम् ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vijayalakṣmi
jaya kamalāsini sadgati dāyini, jñānavikāsini gānamayē
anudina marchita kuṅkuma dhūsara, bhūṣita vāsita vādyanutē ।
kanakadharāstuti vaibhava vandita, śaṅkaradēśika mānyapadē
jaya jayahē madhusūdana kāmini, vijayalakṣmī sadā pālaya mām ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vijaya Lakshmi: Victory to you, O one seated on the lotus, grantor of the good path, revealer of wisdom, of the form of divine song — daily worshipped and adorned with saffron — victory to you, O Devi Lakshmi!

contentBlocks.original:
विद्यालक्ष्मि
प्रणत सुरेश्वरि भारति भार्गवि, शोकविनाशिनि रत्नमये
मणिमय भूषित कर्णविभूषण, शान्ति समावृत हास्यमुखे ।
नवनिधि दायिनि कलिमलहारिणि, कामित फलप्रद हस्तयुते
जय जयहे मधुसूदन कामिनि, विद्यालक्ष्मी सदा पालय माम् ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidyālakṣmi
praṇata surēśvari bhārati bhārgavi, śōkavināśini ratnamayē
maṇimaya bhūṣita karṇavibhūṣaṇa, śānti samāvṛta hāsyamukhē ।
navanidhi dāyini kalimalahāriṇi, kāmita phalaprada hastayutē
jaya jayahē madhusūdana kāmini, vidyālakṣmī sadā pālaya mām ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Vidya Lakshmi: O Bharati (Saraswati), saluted by the gods, O Bhargavi, destroyer of sorrow, of the nature of gems — adorned with jewelled ornaments and ear ornaments, the abode of peace — victory to you, O Devi Lakshmi!

contentBlocks.original:
धनलक्ष्मि
धिमिधिमि धिन्धिमि धिन्धिमि-दिन्धिमि, दुन्धुभि नाद सुपूर्णमये
घुमघुम घुङ्घुम घुङ्घुम घुङ्घुम, शङ्ख निनाद सुवाद्यनुते ।
वेद पूराणेतिहास सुपूजित, वैदिक मार्ग प्रदर्शयुते
जय जयहे मधुसूदन कामिनि, धनलक्ष्मि रूपेणा पालय माम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhanalakṣmi
dhimidhimi dhindhimi dhindhimi-dindhimi, dundhubhi nāda supūrṇamayē
ghumaghuma ghuṅghuma ghuṅghuma ghuṅghuma, śaṅkha nināda suvādyanutē ।
vēda pūrāṇētihāsa supūjita, vaidika mārga pradarśayutē
jaya jayahē madhusūdana kāmini, dhanalakṣmi rūpēṇā pālaya mām ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dhana Lakshmi: With the dundubhi drums resounding in joyful fullness — with the jingling of anklets and bells ringing out — surrounded by all the gods, bearing the conch and lotus — victory to you, O Devi Lakshmi!
